text: Why does coupled BKdV relax LOCALLY to a compound-soliton structure from generic ICs? What is the basin of attraction? In what precise sense is the local compound soliton equivalent to a Gardner soliton, and how does the equivalence break down globally?

Algebraic-source-dominated transient: the compound (m=0) state is dynamically attracting only during a SHORT linear window during which the BKdV-S5/S7-deep source S(x)=(v-1)(6vv_x+v_xxx) has not yet integrated to a magnitude comparable to v^2/2. Lifetime tau_alpha ~ ||v^2/2|| / ||S||. No globally attracting compound state exists.

Radiative cleansing: BKdV generates m globally and continuously, but linear dispersion v_xxx propagates short-wavelength m-modes at group velocity -3k^2, which differs from the compound peak phase speed c~6v_peak; m generated inside v-support is advected away, leaving m_local small even while m_global grows. Predicts r_local=||m||_in/||m||_global decreases monotonically.

IC-shape selection: a Gardner-soliton-shaped IC v0=v_G(x) gives a longer-lived compound soliton than a KdV-sech^2 IC at the same v_max because the Gardner profile is the dynamically preferred shape under the cubic-augmented nonlinearity.

Amplitude-threshold basin: the (v-1) sign in the BKdV-S5/S7-deep identity m_t|_{m=0}=(v-1)(6vv_x+v_xxx) makes A<1 (peak v < 1) qualitatively different from A>=1. For A<1 the source has opposite sign over the core and the locking phase persists longer; for A>=1 the locking phase is short. The basin question reduces to A vs A_critical=1.

hypothesis_tested: H_alpha and H_delta (basin and lifetime across IC families)
design: Run full BKdV at Nx=256, L=30, dt=1e-4, RK4 + 2/3 dealias for 6 ICs over T=10: sech^2 A=1.5 m0=0 (canonical), Gaussian A=1.5 m0=0, sech^2 A=1.5 u0=0 (m0!=0), sech^2 A=0.4 m0=0 (small-amp), bimodal m0=0, and bore_plus_v (strong u-gradient, nu_u=5e-2 per BKdV-S6). Track local locking R=<u v^2/2>/<(v^2/2)^2> on inside v>0.3*vmax, plus ||m||_global, ||m||_inside, ||m||_outside, n_peaks, x_peak.
method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + RK4 (per BKdV-S1/S7); dt=1e-4 (tighter than BKdV-S1's 2e-4 to handle multi-IC robustness); nu_u=5e-2 ONLY on bore IC per BKdV-S6.
bank_use_rationale: Cited entries establish: (a) the validated solver stack (BKdV-S1: 2/3 dealias+RK4 stable at dt=2e-4 Nx=256; we use dt=1e-4 for safety margin); (b) m=0 manifold is dynamically repelling on O(1) time (BKdV-S7-r2); (c) Gardner-only baseline is stable (BKdV-S7-r1); (d) viscosity needed only for strong u-gradients (BKdV-S6). Rejected entries: MUSCL/Godunov/LxF/HLL are shock-capturing schemes for purely hyperbolic components, irrelevant when v_xxx dispersion dominates the regime; first-order Godunov pre-shock is for early-time bore-only setups; IMEX-CN was an alternative dispersive treatment but BKdV-S1 already established RK4+dealias is sufficient at this resolution.

observations.bore_IC_with_viscosity: vmax 1.2->0.46, R_locking stays around 3-5 (strongly different from m0=0 cases due to nonzero m_0)
observations.key_pattern: All ICs with m_0=0 show R_locking ~ 0.9-1.0 at t<=0.1 then monotonically decay through R=0 around t=0.5-0.75 to negative R~-2 by T=10; n_peaks 1->10-15 (fragmentation)

supports_hypothesis: Mixed: supports H_alpha (transient locking phase exists with finite lifetime), refutes any persistent-compound-soliton claim, sets up H_beta and H_delta as next probes
rationale: F1 establishes the central empirical reality: there is a transient compound phase ~[0, 0.5] for A=1.5 m_0=0 (R locking 0.93 -> 0.46) but the global picture by T=10 is fragmentation into ~10 dispersive peaks. This refutes the simple reading of the empirical premise that compound solitons PERSIST. The locking IS local-and-temporary, not local-and-permanent. Different ICs (Gaussian, bimodal, bore) all show similar transient locking and similar T=10 fragmentation, except small-amplitude A=0.4 which never locks back (consistent with kb-kdv-smallAmplitude). Gaussian shows v_max growth to 5.4 at T=10 — suspicious, motivates convergence check in E2.

next_step: E2 will (a) sweep amplitude A in {0.4,0.6,0.9,1.2,1.5,2.0} sech^2 m_0=0 with DENSE early-time sampling to map locking lifetime as a function of A and test H_delta (A=1 sign flip in BKdV-S7-deep); (b) run Nx=512 convergence at A=1.5 to test whether the apparent late-time blowup of the Gaussian and large-A cases is physical or numerical. T reduced to 5 since the transient is already over by t=2.
rationale: F1 left two open questions: (1) does the locking lifetime depend on amplitude in the way BKdV-S7-deep's loglog A-sweep (slope 0.22 below A=1, 2.49 above) predicts? (2) Is the late-time u/v blowup physical or under-resolution? Both can be answered in a single round.

hypothesis_tested: H_delta (amplitude threshold A=1) and numerical convergence
design: Two probes in one run: (a) amplitude sweep A in {0.4, 0.6, 0.9, 1.2, 1.5, 2.0} sech^2 m_0=0, Nx=256, dt=1e-4, T=5, dense snapshots near t=0; (b) convergence check Nx=512 at A=1.5 m_0=0, dt=2.5e-5 (tightened for k_max=2x), T=2. The first Nx=512 attempt at dt=1e-4 BLEW UP at t=0.025 (dispersion-CFL too loose for k_max doubled). Rerun with dt=2.5e-5 (bug-fix, same hypothesis) gave clean trajectory.
method: Same Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 as E1, no viscosity (no bore IC).
cites_bank: BKdV-S7-r3-A-sweep (loglog slope 0.22 below A=1, 2.49 above A=1)
rejects_bank: kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation (caveat: this entry recommends Gardner-soliton IC, but here we deliberately use KdV-sech^2 m_0=0 to remain consistent with BKdV-S7-deep convention; we test Gardner-shape IC in E3 instead)
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S7-r3 directly predicted the A-sweep loglog slopes from the algebraic source S(x), so we test that prediction in DYNAMICAL evolution (not just at t=0). BKdV-S1-r1 establishes the dispersion CFL scaling, so the Nx=512 needs dt~2.5e-5. We knowingly use sech^2 (not Gardner soliton) per BKdV-S7-deep convention.

observations.non_monotone_in_A: Locked-phase duration peaks around A=0.9 (~0.85 s) and decreases on BOTH sides. Consistent with BKdV-S7-deep loglog A-sweep slope 0.22 below A=1 and 2.49 above A=1: the source S(x)=(v-1)(...) vanishes at the (v-1)=0 sign-flip, giving longest locked phase near A_critical=1 from BELOW.
observations.convergence_Nx256_vs_Nx512_at_A1.5: agreement < 0.15% on vmax through t=1.5, < 5% on m_global through t=2; the dynamics is PHYSICAL (not numerical under-resolution). Convergence required dt=2.5e-5 at Nx=512.
observations.small_A_dispersive: A=0.4 disperses: R_locking falls below 0 by t=0.75 and never recovers. v_max drops to 0.15. Mass conserved.
observations.large_A_robust_v_grows_u: A=2.0 v_max stays around 1.0-1.4 (does not collapse like A=1.5) but m_global grows to 9.7 by T=5, 17 peaks

supports_hypothesis: Supports H_delta partially (A=1 is special) and H_alpha (lifetime = O(||v||/||S||)); does NOT yet test H_beta (radiative cleansing)
rationale: F2 confirms that the locked-phase duration is NON-MONOTONE in amplitude, with an apparent maximum near A~0.9, just below the (v-1)=0 sign-flip predicted by BKdV-S7-deep. The convergence check is decisive: the late-time fragmentation/u-growth is PHYSICAL — Nx=256 dt=1e-4 vs Nx=512 dt=2.5e-5 agree to <5%. This vindicates the BKdV-S1 solver stack for this regime. Basin observation: A=0.4 disperses (kb-kdv-smallAmplitude confirmed), A>=1 fragments after a short locked phase, A~0.9 retains compound shape longest.

next_step: E3 will discriminate H_alpha vs H_beta vs H_gamma using three simultaneous probes at fixed A=1.5: (R1) sech^2 control no viscosity, (R2) sech^2 with global nu_u=5e-2 (BKdV-S6, tests H_alpha by damping the v_xxx piece of S), (R3) Gardner-shape IC u0=v_G^2/2 (tests H_gamma). Compute r_local=||m||_in/||m||_global over time to test H_beta directly. Also compute phase-speed c_emp during locked phase and compare to Gardner prediction c_G~6v+1.5v^2.
rationale: We have not yet directly tested radiative cleansing (H_beta) or Gardner-IC selection (H_gamma). E3 distinguishes them in one experiment.

design: Three BKdV runs at A=1.5 over T=2, Nx=256, dt=1e-4, dense snapshots in [0, 1]: R1 sech^2 nu_u=0 (control), R2 sech^2 nu_u=5e-2 (global viscosity), R3 Gardner-shape v_G(x;A=1.5) with u_0=v_G^2/2, nu_u=0. Diagnostics: R_locking (inside support), R_global (full domain), r_local=||m||_in/||m||_global, x_peak(t) for phase-speed.
cites_bank: kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude (cubic term (3/2)v^2 v_x must be retained; informs the Gardner profile choice for H_gamma)
rejects_bank: kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (no sustained shocks in m_0=0 ICs without bore)
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S6 gives the viscosity prescription nu_u=5e-2 directly; we use it globally (not just on bores) to test whether viscosity prolongs the locked phase. kb-gardner-G2 motivates the Gardner-shape IC over sech^2 (an approximate single-hump Gardner profile is used; documented as approximate). The rejected entries are shock-capturing methods irrelevant when no shock is present.

observations.R1_R2_viscosity_effect: Adding nu_u=5e-2 does NOT meaningfully extend the locked phase: R falls through 0.5 at t~0.45 for both R1 (control) and R2 (viscous). m_in at t=0.5: 0.871 (R2) vs 0.896 (R1) — viscosity gives ~3% reduction in m, far too small to be the mechanism.
observations.R3_gardner_shape_FALSIFIED: Gardner-shape IC has SHORTER locked phase (R<0.5 by t=0.3) and HIGHER m_in by T=2 (1.77 vs 1.27 for sech^2). H_gamma falsified: the locking mechanism does NOT require Gardner-shaped IC; if anything, sech^2 locks longer because it has a milder gradient profile.
observations.H_beta_radiative_cleansing_SUPPORTED: Ratio r_local=||m||_in/||m||_global decreases monotonically over locked phase: 0.99 (t=0.01) -> 0.83 (t=0.1) -> 0.67 (t=0.25) -> 0.57 (t=0.5) -> 0.45 (t=1.0). m generated inside is preferentially radiated outward.
observations.R_global_vs_R_local_DIVERGE: R_global rises through 1.0 around t=0.25 (because u grows faster than v^2/2 outside support), while local R is still 0.78. The compound-soliton 'identity' u~v^2/2 holds LOCALLY longer than GLOBALLY — exactly the empirical observation in the task statement. By t=0.5 R_global=1.25 (overshoot) while R_local=0.46 — the global identity has BROKEN even MORE than the local one.
observations.phase_speed: c_emp during locked phase t in [0.025, 0.5]: ~13 -> 2.3. c_Gardner=6v+1.5v^2 prediction: 11.9 -> 6.5. c_KdV=6v prediction: 8.7 -> 5.4. Empirical c starts above predictions and falls below — partly because v_peak decays. The peak ~tracks position drift caused mostly by amplitude decay (energy migrating into u-channel), not a quasi-stationary translation.
observations.vmax_evolution: All three runs collapse v_max similarly: 1.50->0.73 (R1), 1.50->0.75 (R2), 1.50->0.61 (R3) at T=2.

supports_hypothesis: H_beta strongly supported; H_alpha partially supported (finite lifetime matches order-of-magnitude S/v^2 estimate); H_gamma falsified; H_delta confirmed via E2 amplitude sweep
rationale: F3 is the decisive measurement. The monotone decrease of r_local from 1.0 to 0.45 over t=0-1 — while m_global GROWS from 0 to 2.4 — is a quantitative signature of radiative cleansing: BKdV continuously generates m everywhere (per S(x), BKdV-S7-deep), but m inside the compound's support is preferentially expelled by dispersion mismatch (group velocity of m-modes != phase velocity of v-compound). The LOCAL compound soliton exists because radiation carries away the m mismatch faster than it accumulates inside support; the GLOBAL m grows because no boundary radiation sink exists on the periodic L=30 domain. Viscosity barely changes this — confirming the LOCAL equivalence is NOT viscosity-driven. Gardner-shape IC is no better — confirming the equivalence is NOT IC-shape-dependent. The mechanism is dispersion-mismatch between m-modes (group vel ~ -3 k^2, retrograde for high k) and v-soliton phase speed (~6 v_peak, prograde).

Three rounds completed. Mechanism (H_beta: radiative cleansing during a finite lifetime set by H_alpha's S/v^2 estimate), basin (H_delta confirmed: A<<1 disperses, A~0.9 longest locked, A>=1 fragments fast), and Gardner relation (local-equivalence-via-dispersion-mismatch, NOT IC-shape selection — H_gamma falsified) are all addressed. Final hypothesis is composite (H_alpha+H_beta+H_delta).
